lesson: Cross-process in-memory state (_update_auth_state) does not persist. Must write to google_identity.json via _push_identity_to_server for the HTTP /auth endpoint to see the email.
context: Welcome tab showed 'Signed in' instead of username because CLI process updated only in-memory state

lesson: Always scan existing tabs after full_reboot before creating new ones. Chrome may auto-spawn sign-in pages, causing duplicates.
context: require_tab opened a second sign-in tab after window recovery because it didn't check for auto-spawned tabs

lesson: Background daemon threads (_login_tracker_loop) can open tabs in parallel with CLI flow, causing duplicates. Use start_tracker=False when CLI handles polling.
context: Two sign-in tabs appeared because _login_tracker_loop probed identity while CLI was waiting for login

lesson: rm -rf symlink/ (with trailing slash) follows through and deletes the target directory contents. Always use rm symlink (no slash) to remove symlinks.
context: User deleted .cursor/skills/ symlinks via trailing-slash rm, which destroyed all project skill files

lesson: CLI commands with extensibility potential must use sub-options (sub-commands) where each implementation is a separate sub-option. New implementations become new sub-options, not flags.
context: SKILLS market originally used --source flag; restructured to SKILLS market <source> <action> for extensibility

lesson: Subagent policy violation: launched 3 subagents for documentation work that should have been done inline. Even when model:fast was specified, subagents should only be used for genuinely parallel independent tasks. Sequential blocking work must be done inline. Also, always verify subagent model parameter is honored.
context: User caught that 3 subagents for logic/ DFS documentation ran blocking/sequential instead of truly parallel. Rule says: prefer inline work over subagents unless 2+ independent tasks benefit from parallelism.

lesson: Subagent policy violation (repeated): Used default model instead of model:fast, and launched subagents sequentially (blocking) instead of in parallel. The rule clearly states: (1) ONLY use model:fast, (2) subagents MUST run in parallel — if a task is sequential, do it inline. Root cause: rushing through documentation tasks and defaulting to subagent delegation without checking the policy. Fix: always re-read the subagent-policy rule before launching any subagent. When in doubt, do the work inline.

When building tool GUIs, always check logic/gui/tkinter/blueprint/ first for reusable patterns. OPENCLAW chatbot GUI was built custom when it should have been a blueprint in the shared directory, reusable by other tools. The avoid-duplicate-implementations skill covers code but not GUI architecture.

When building tool integrations that depend on Chrome tabs, always check logic/chrome/session.py for open_tab() and CDMCP's tab management capabilities. OPENCLAW pipeline gave up when Yuanbao tab wasn't found instead of using open_tab() to create one. The task-orchestration and recipes skills cover this (CDMCP Session Bootstrap recipe) but were not consulted during implementation.

CDMCP provides boot_tool_session() and ensure_chrome() for Chrome lifecycle management. NEVER manually check is_chrome_cdp_available() and give up — use ensure_chrome() which auto-relaunches. NEVER write custom tab opening logic — use session.require_tab(). Read tool/GOOGLE.CDMCP/for_agent.md BEFORE building any Chrome-dependent tool.

OpenClaw does NOT auto-formulate skills from errors. Evolution comes from: (1) mandatory skill scanning in system prompt, (2) memory_search for recall, (3) user-editable workspace files (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md). Our Error->Lesson->Rule->Skill->Hook pipeline is actually ahead of OpenClaw.

OpenClaw state packaging = structured system prompt (not raw context dump) + bootstrap workspace files + skill catalog + memory search + runtime info. Key: skills are MANDATORY pre-scan before every reply.

BILIBILI filter-search with --sort views is the most efficient way to find high-view videos. Regular search returns mixed results. Use broad keywords like year (2025) or category (搞笑) for best coverage.

Gmail SPA requires full mouse event chain (mousedown+mouseup+click via dispatchEvent) for checkbox/button interactions. A bare .click() is silently swallowed. Always use _gmail_click() helper which dispatches the full chain.

For full-stack web apps, use Flask+SQLite for backend (zero-config) and Vite+React for frontend. Use --cache /tmp/npm-cache if npm _cacache has permission issues. Flask CORS needs supports_credentials=True and explicit origins for session cookies.

HTML chatbot blueprint at logic/gui/html/blueprint/chatbot/ provides a browser-based alternative to tkinter chatbot. No Tcl dependency, better styling (CSS), WebSocket for real-time updates with HTTP polling fallback. Use ChatbotServer for web-based chat GUIs.

Yuanbao (yuanbao.tencent.com) uses Quill editor (.ql-editor contenteditable), not textarea. Set innerHTML directly with

tags and dispatch input event. Send button is an with class style__send-btn, clickable via full MouseEvent chain. Response selector is .hyc-common-markdown. New chat icon: [class*=ic_newchat].

Many modern SPAs (Gmail, Yuanbao) require full MouseEvent chain (mousedown+mouseup+click via dispatchEvent) for button/checkbox interactions. A bare el.click() is swallowed. CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent may also fail due to coordinate issues. Always use JS dispatchEvent with bubbles:true.

SPA frameworks (Gmail, Yuanbao, Figma) ignore bare .click() calls. Always use full MouseEvent chain: mousedown -> mouseup -> click with {bubbles: true, cancelable: true, view: window}. Also, Input.insertText via CDP is more reliable than innerHTML for text injection in contenteditable editors.

Python stdout buffering: when a process blocks indefinitely (e.g. server.wait()), stdout is never flushed in non-TTY contexts. Always use flush=True on print calls before blocking, or set PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1.
CDMCP open_tab() returns False on failure rather than raising an exception. Functions that call it must check the return value explicitly, not rely on try/except alone for fallback logic.

HTML GUI session items: click handlers must be on the parent row element (session-item), not inner child divs (session-title). Also add role=button, tabindex=0, and aria-label for accessibility and CDMCP automation compatibility.
tool.json.tmpl template: JSON braces ({}) must be doubled ({{ }}) to avoid KeyError with Python str.format(). Only placeholder {name} uses single braces.
在异常处理中使用 sys.exit() 时必须传入退出码参数。sys.exit() 无参数会使用当前线程的退出码，导致错误码不可预测。应始终使用 sys.exit(code) 明确指定退出码。

When creating new root-level directories (hooks/, .cursor/), always check .gitignore first. This project uses an deny-by-default pattern (/* at line 2) that requires explicit !/<dir>/ entries for each tracked root directory.
context: Created hooks/interface/AI-IDE/Cursor/ and .cursor/hooks.json but they were gitignored. User had to remind to check gitignore.
